name: neurips_submission_data
description: Model responses, rendered answer text, study metadata, and PNG stimuli.
citation: NeurIPS submission data assembled from the chang_neutral workspace.
rai:dataLimitations:
This dataset is intended for research and evaluation of AI responses to U.S. political issues, and not necessarily for deployment decisions or wider claims about political neutrality in every context. 
One limitation is that the dataset is geographically and culturally scoped to the United States: everything from the political issues to the participant sample chosen are all US-centered and US-based, so generalizability to other countries, languages, political systems, and cultural contexts is not guaranteed. Furthermore, the 20 political issues chosen are values-based, and this dataset is not intended to evaluate all forms of ideological disagreement or factual disagreements. Finally, the dataset reflects design choices in prompt and response construction, as user prompts were sourced primarily from Reddit or Reddit-based datasets and may not represent all real-world questions or conversational contexts. Model responses were generated from a fixed set of frontier models at a particular point in time and should not be treated as representative of all AI systems, behaviors, or response styles. The main metric of human approval and the different approval statements are important for studying trust and perceived neutrality but are not necessarily equivalent to factual accuracy or moral correctness.
This dataset should not be used for individual-level profiling, political targeting or persuasion, or to infer political identities. It should not be used to train systems that aim to persuade people’s politics or manipulate users. It should not be used as evidence that a model is completely safe for real-world political deployment, as a universal benchmark of perfectly “unbiased”, “fair”, or “neutral” AI systems, or as a substitution for purpose-driven, human-centered evaluation. Users of this dataset should not perform analyses that increase re-identification risk or make claims about small subgroups without the appropriate precautions.
rai:dataBiases: There are several known and suspected sources of bias in this dataset. First, the human evaluations come from US-based Prolific participants, so the dataset reflects their views, norms, and platform-specific patterns rather than a truly globally representative sample. The recruitment process intentionally balanced participants across broad political affiliation categories, with 45% conservative, 15% moderate, and 40% liberal participants, oversampling on the self-identified conservative ideology in order to obtain sufficient coverage across issue positions. This recruitment strategy should not be interpreted as producing a nationally representative sample. The resulting participant pool was mostly female, skewed younger, and approximately half of participants held full-time employment. While broad ethnicity categories mirrored that of the U.S., these demographic patterns may affect observed approval ratings and may not reflect how other populations would evaluate the same AI responses. Since the issues, canonical questions, Reddit questions, and model responses are all based in the US context, models trained or evaluated using this dataset may therefore learn patterns of approval that are specific to U.S. political discourse.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation:
Political beliefs or opinions: Participants reported their positions on controversial U.S. political issues and rated AI responses to those issues. This was critical for the purpose of the dataset construction, which was to study pluralistic neutrality in the US context.
Gender and Age: The study collected or used demographic attributes including age, sex or gender-related information, ethnicity, employment status, student status, and broad political affiliation labels used for recruitment and analysis. This was important to see how different demographic groups react, and to give a baseline to compare population realism against the real world. For example, it is well established that voters in different age groups behave in heterogeneous ways. 
Geography: Participants were recruited from the United States, so country-level geographic information is part of the dataset context. This was important because of the scope of the study, which was to study these questions in the context of American culture and politics.
Socio-economic status, Experience or seniority: Employment status and student status may serve as partial proxies for socio-economic position. Including these attributes was important to see how different groups react, and to give a baseline to compare population realism against the real world.
Language and culture: The dataset is in English and reflects U.S. political and cultural contexts. Again, this is important due to the scope of the study being in the US.
Open-ended user-generated content: Free-text feedback may contain incidental personal disclosures even if direct identifiers were not intentionally collected. We made an effort to automatically as well as manually review user-generated content for personally identifiable information, and did not release the responses we identified as having personally identifiable information. The dataset should not include direct identifiers such as participant names, email addresses, IP addresses, or platform-given participant IDs in public release files. Our quality and privacy checks to free-text responses included LLM-assisted screening and manual review for responses that could create re-identification risk. However, residual privacy risk may remain because political opinions, demographic attributes, and open-ended text can be sensitive when combined together and disaggregated. Users should avoid attempts to re-identify participants, link records to external data, or report results for very small subgroups.
rai:dataUseCases: This dataset was collected to evaluate Americans’ approval of various types of AI responses to controversial political questions. It is intended to represent human judgments of AI responses in politically sensitive, values-based disagreement contexts, including perceived approval, fairness, bias against the participant’s view, quality of summarization, and inclusion of the participant’s view. It can be used for additional similar analyses, or to train models to approximate human responses to such questions.
rai:dataSocialImpact:
This dataset could have positive social impact by allowing for more rigorous evaluation of how AI systems respond to controversial, values-based political questions. This dataset provides a way to study whether or not AI responses can earn approval from people on opposite sides of a political issue instead of only optimizing for one group or relying on self-reported commentary from the model itself. This in turn could support research on political neutrality, pluralistic alignment, fairness, trust, and responsible deployment of AI systems in sensitive or hotly contested contexts. However, because this dataset contains political opinions and evaluations of persuasive or balanced political responses, it could be misused to assist in the training of systems that optimize for political persuasion or manipulation. Taken a step further, it could also be misused to infer political identities, profile demographic groups, or overstate a model’s readiness for real-world political deployment. Strong performance on this dataset should not be treated as proof that a model is universally neutral, truthful, safe, or fair across all contexts.
Mitigations should include clear documentation of limitations when the dataset is used, de-identification of participant data whenever applicable, careful inspection and redaction of free-text responses that could create re-identification risk, and explicit usage guidance against the potential negative effects above. If sensitive free-text or demographic fields are released, there should be stringent access controls and usage terms.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.prov:label: Question-answer pair extraction from reddit1M and eli5
prov:wasGeneratedBy.sc:description: Downloaded the datasets from huggingface, collecting over one million questions users asked on reddit.

prov:wasAttributedTo.prov:label: Research Team
prov:wasAttributedTo.sc:description: Two members of our research team downloaded this data from huggingface.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.prov:label: Manual search on Reddit
prov:wasGeneratedBy.sc:description: For issues that were less well-represented in the datasets, we manually searched for Reddit threads that were related to the topical issue (eg. abortion, gun_control, etc).

prov:wasAttributedTo.prov:label: Research Team
prov:wasAttributedTo.sc:description: Two members of our research team searched for questions on Reddit.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.prov:label: Valence classification
prov:wasGeneratedBy.sc:description: For each issue, we collected 10 from the three reddit sources (eli5, reddit1M, manual search), two from each of the five valences: {Strongly For, For, Neutral, Against, Strongly Against} (with respect to the policy proposed in the canonical survey question).

prov:wasAttributedTo.prov:label: Research Team
prov:wasAttributedTo.sc:description: All members of the research team were involved in valence classification - first the initial collection was done by two members, and final review of the valence classification for user prompts was done by another two members.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.prov:label: Free text response quality review
prov:wasGeneratedBy.sc:description: We use GPT-5.4-mini to take a piece of free-text feedback and annotate its quality. We dropped rows with low quality free text responses.

prov:wasAttributedTo.prov:label: Research Team
prov:wasAttributedTo.sc:description: All members of the research team reviewed the low quality free text responses and unilaterally agreed to exclude these participants from our dataset.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.prov:label: Regex + LLM Relevance
prov:wasGeneratedBy.sc:description: We use GPT-4o-mini and per-topic regexes to classify whether a question within the reddit1M or ELI5 dataset would be a relevant paraphrase of our canonical question.

prov:wasAttributedTo.prov:label: Research Team
prov:wasAttributedTo.sc:description: The research team members (2) supervised the LLM and interpreted its results.
